settings.modelMemoryProfileHint: Controls how aggressively models are unloaded after calls: strict memory saving, balanced mode, or max speed.
settings.defineCharacterBehavior: Define the character's behavior and personality...

moralSettings.title: Moral Matrix Settings
moralSettings.llamaCppEnabledHint: Off by default — kept separate from active-provider selection as a safety guard.
moralSettings.fallbackHint: The execution chain runs the active provider first, then enabled fallbacks in order.
moralSettings.decayHint: Once a day each EmotionalTrace's intensity is multiplied by (1 − global_rate), bounded by persistence_floor.
moralSettings.forgivenessHint: When recent messages carry compensating tones from the user, old emotional traces soften — but never below persistence_floor.
moralSettings.scarsHint: Triggers that promote an EmotionalTrace to an elevated persistence_floor — decay and forgiveness cannot lower it past that. First matching trigger wins.
moralSettings.innerVoiceHint: One short first-person sentence explaining the feeling. Adds one mini LLM call per turn — disable if latency matters.

ragSettings.memoryConsolidationHint: Runs during nightly sleep-consolidation after diary generation. Does NOT affect per-turn pipeline.
ragSettings.consolidationImportanceHint: Diary entries with importance_score below this are flagged pruned and hidden by default. 0.0 disables the filter.
ragSettings.consolidationJudgeHint: Optional. One LLM call per diary entry with non-empty contradictions[]. Decides supersede / merge / keep_both / skip. Runs ONLY during nightly consolidation, never per-turn.
ragSettings.consolidationJudgeModelHint: Model list comes from the selected provider. If you use llama.cpp, leave this blank — the model is picked from the base_url server.
ragSettings.narrativeDiaryHint: Adds a `narrative` field to the diary JSON response — 200-400 words in first person, in the user's language. NO extra LLM call — same generation pass as the structured payload.

audioSettings.sttHint: Pick the STT engine. faster-whisper is the default.
audioSettings.sherpaHint: Local STT via ONNX Runtime. Weights live in backend/storage/models/stt/. Paths below are relative to that directory.
audioSettings.sherpaPathHint: Relative path to the file (e.g. model.onnx).

voiceSettings.qwenHint: Local Qwen3-TTS via transformers. Requires a GPU (~6-8GB VRAM for bfloat16). Weights live in backend/storage/models/qwen/. First load downloads from HuggingFace.
voiceSettings.qwenModelName: Model name (HuggingFace ID or local path)
voiceSettings.qwenModelHint: Default: Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-Flash. You can also point this at a local snapshot.

complianceSettings.title: Output compliance checks
complianceSettings.intro: All 5 checks run after generation in sync mode. Streaming is not validated. The output is never modified — results land in runtime_meta and the audit log. Auto-reroll is deferred — for now we detect, not block.
complianceSettings.validatorHint: LLM-as-judge: compares output against instructor's directives. compliance < threshold → DebugVault entry. One extra LLM call per sync generation.
complianceSettings.languageGuardHint: CPU-only unicode-script counter (no LLM). Compares the output's dominant script with User.language. On mismatch → DebugVault entry.
complianceSettings.langMinDominanceHint: 0.7 = consider the language determined when 70%+ letters belong to one script. Lower — skip (legitimate mixed-language).
complianceSettings.langMinOutputCharsHint: Short outputs (under this) are skipped — not enough data for reliable detection.
complianceSettings.confidenceHint: Mini LLM call: scores 0-1 how confident PAI should be the output addressed the question. Score lands in runtime_meta.confidence. Low → audit WARNING (NOT DebugVault — it's a signal).
complianceSettings.confidenceThresholdHint: Below this score the output is flagged low confidence. Factuality uses this as a gate.
complianceSettings.factualityHint: CPU-only: regex extracts claims (years / dates / numbers+units / capitalized phrases) and looks them up in the lorebook. No web fallback. Output is not modified — writes to runtime_meta.factuality.
complianceSettings.factualityGate: Run only when confidence is low
complianceSettings.factualityGateHint: Recommended. Otherwise the check runs on every output for no reason — wastes CPU.
complianceSettings.selfWatcherHint: Observation-only. Compares PAI's predicted emotion on turn N-1 with the user's actual tone on turn N. Mismatch → expectation_events. Nightly LLM aggregates them into the diary's self_reflection.
complianceSettings.swMismatchHint: Score ≥ threshold inserts an expectation_events row. Below — ignored. 0.5 = opposite valences (positive vs negative).
complianceSettings.swNightlyHint: Once a day an LLM clusters recent N events → writes to daily_activity_diary.payload.self_reflection. One LLM call per day per character.

systemSettings.uiLanguageHint: Affects UI localization, debug logs, and system text only. Does NOT control the language PAI speaks in.
systemSettings.generationLanguageHint: The actual language PAI replies in. Used by resolve_user_language and threaded into every LLM call (diary, inner voice, reflection, etc.).
systemSettings.auditRetentionHint: Nightly prune enforces per-severity policy. Rows older than age_days are deleted. When hard_cap is exceeded, oldest rows are deleted first.
systemSettings.auditAgeHint: Rows of the given severity are deleted once they reach this age. 0 = delete immediately.
systemSettings.auditCapHint: When more rows of this severity exist, the oldest are deleted down to this number. 0 = no cap.
systemSettings.modeEnvHint: audit_logs verbosity is controlled by the MODE env var at backend startup: MODE=dev (default) writes everything. MODE=prod drops info/success. NOT manageable via UI — changing it requires a restart.

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.subtitle: Isolated prompt and model testing without writing to chat history.
sandbox.unifiedRouter: Use unified pass to review generated image
sandbox.processEmpty: Run a test to inspect pipeline steps.

auth.anonymousModeHint: No personal settings or API keys

coreSettings.title: Core Settings
coreSettings.dlDescription: Configure decision-making and logic processing modules
coreSettings.caDescription: Configure cognitive analysis and reasoning capabilities
coreSettings.instructorBuildSchemaHint: Template for final context assembly. Available placeholders: {core}, {rules}, {context}, {memory}, {perception}, {self_state}.
coreSettings.excludeDisabledModulesHint: When enabled, disabled modules are omitted from the final context. When disabled, the instructor may add diagnostic [ERROR] blocks for required but unavailable modules.
coreSettings.routingModeHint: System mode uses rules and analyzer data. Intelligent mode asks the selected LLM to make routing decisions through a tool-call.
coreSettings.useExternalApi: Use External API (instead of local model)
coreSettings.fallbackToLocal: Fallback to Local Model if API fails

matrix.title: Moral Matrix
matrix.subtitle: Spectral analysis and current PAI state

memory.title: Memory Vault
memory.subtitle: Search memory traces by phrase and exact message UUID
memory.emptyText: Try another phrase or an exact message UUID.

synthesis.title: Synthesis Hub
synthesis.subtitle: UI preview for media generation (without backend binding)

personaSettings.title: 👤 Persona Settings
personaSettings.name: Name
personaSettings.telegramBindingsHint: When a chat is bound to a persona, Telegram history, replies, and image generation use that persona. Empty value keeps the shared active fallback.
personaSettings.telegramBindingsEmpty: No chats loaded yet. Start Telegram module or refresh chats in social settings.

mediaSettings.title: 🎨 Media Generation Settings
mediaSettings.activeProviderHint: Main chat, sandbox, and Telegram use this provider when route is auto.
mediaSettings.comfyuiModelsHint: ComfyUI models are not loaded. Check ComfyUI API and refresh.
mediaSettings.characterAppearancePlaceholder: Describe stable appearance: hair, eyes, style, age, mood, signature traits.
mediaSettings.appearanceRootPlaceholder: Main identity anchor (hair, eyes, signature traits, style).
mediaSettings.imageScenariosHint: Scenarios define soft hints and defaults for sandbox, Telegram, and main chat. Empty fields fall back to the shared provider or base settings.
mediaSettings.videoAudioHint: Reserved section for video/audio providers. Image stack is already split here.

diary.title: Diary
diary.subtitle: Personal AI notes and session reflections

audit.title: Audit Journal
audit.subtitle: Diagnostic view of pipeline behavior and quality signals

communication.channelPriorityHint: Defines which channel is treated as primary. If Telegram is primary, the core can fall back to main chat only when Telegram fails.
communication.telegramFallback: Use main chat as fallback on Telegram failure

modules.title: Social Modules
modules.hint: These are top-level source toggles. They enable the social contours themselves, while detailed Telegram behavior is configured below.
modules.twitchReserved: Twitch screen is reserved for the next step.

telegram.bridgeHint: Configure Telegram account or bot access, chat catalog loading, and the base bridge limits here.
telegram.apiIdHint: Required for MTProto account login. Issued on my.telegram.org.
telegram.phoneHint: Used for MTProto account login. Format: +79991234567.
telegram.botTokenHint: Only used in bot mode. Not needed for MTProto account mode.
telegram.queueSizeHint: How many incoming events the bridge can buffer before the notification worker processes them.
telegram.historyMaxMessagesHint: How many recent messages are pulled into Telegram dialog context.

catalog.title: Telegram Chats Catalog
catalog.hint: After the bridge starts, you can load visible private/group/channel peers and choose them without manually typing chat_id.
catalog.empty: No chats loaded yet. Start the bridge and refresh the catalog.

allowlist.title: Allowed Chats (Reply Allowlist)
allowlist.hint: List of chats the bridge may reply to at all. This is an early filter before the final write policy.

writePolicy.title: Private Delivery Policy
writePolicy.hint: Final runtime write gate. Even if the model or a tool tries to send something, the sender still obeys these rules.
writePolicy.sandboxHint: Use this for safe test delivery without mixing test messages into the owner chat.

routing.title: Routing & Write Policy
routing.hint: Additional behavioral restrictions for the bridge before the final sender-level gate.
routing.readOnlyNonPrivate: Read-only for groups/channels (no writes outside DMs)

channels.title: Channel Read Behavior
channels.hint: Controls how the PAI reads public sources: whether to read posts, mark them as read, and build reflections from them.
channels.reflectionInstructionHint: A short rule describing how to interpret a post before generating a private reflection.
channels.reflectionInstructionDefault: Briefly extract the main facts and possible implications of this public post.

reflection.title: Public Reflection Delivery
reflection.hint: Public channels and groups are read-only sources here. Nothing is sent back into the public source: the result goes only to the private target chat.
reflection.targetChatHint: The PAI sends private thoughts about public posts here. Normally this is the owner chat.
reflection.sourceSelectionHint: If nothing is selected manually, the PAI will use all eligible sources of the enabled kinds after the catalog is loaded.
reflection.sourceSelectionAutomatic: Automatic source mode is active: all available channels and groups of the enabled kinds are used.
reflection.noSourcesLoaded: Available channels and groups are not loaded yet.
reflection.noSourcesLoadedHint: Start the Telegram bridge and refresh the chat catalog. After that, this section will show selectable sources without manual chat_id input.
reflection.promptHint: This is a system instruction for private owner-facing thoughts, not a reply to the public source.
reflection.promptDefault: Read the public Telegram post below and produce a short private reflection for the owner. Do not address the source chat and do not compose a public reply. Summarize what happened, what caught the PAI's attention, and why it may matter.

quietHours.title: Quiet Hours
quietHours.hint: During quiet hours, outward initiative is blocked. The PAI may still read, store, and prepare context, but should not wake the owner.

antiRepeat.title: Repeat Guard
antiRepeat.hint: Controls post-generation duplicate blocking behavior. For direct dialog replies it is recommended to keep strict blocking disabled.
antiRepeat.enforceIncomingDialogs: Enforce repeat blocking for direct incoming dialog replies
antiRepeat.retryOnBlock: Retry generation when blocked by repeat guard

initiative.title: Initiative Scheduler
initiative.hint: Configure proactive cadence, digest windows, and soft pacing rules without turning initiative into a hard kill-switch.
initiative.softDailyTargetHint: This is not a hard limit. The value only stretches the cadence and suppresses initiative frequency.
initiative.bootstrapFromCatalog: Bootstrap tracked chats from Telegram catalog on bridge start
initiative.promptTemplateHint: Used as the base instruction for proactive messages together with runtime time-awareness and open-loop context.
initiative.promptTemplateDefault: There has been no contact in this chat for {idle_minutes} minutes. If appropriate, send one short warm proactive message.

autonomousInbox.title: Autonomous Inbox
autonomousInbox.hint: Background cycle where the PAI can inspect unread events, choose candidates, and decide what to read or open next.
autonomousInbox.privatePauseProbabilityHint: System action selector. Below threshold it pauses/marks read more often; otherwise it replies.
autonomousInbox.promptTemplateDefault: You are online and see unread Telegram events. Choose the next action: open a chat, answer, read a channel, or pause.

lockdown.title: Owner Chat Lock
lockdown.hint: Hard mode where the bridge focuses only on one selected owner chat.

orchestration.title: Tool Orchestration
orchestration.hint: Rules for tool-centric Telegram flows. You can force the model to perform a tool-call before composing the final reply.
orchestration.requireToolCall: Require at least one tool-call before final answer

imageSelector.title: System Image Selector
imageSelector.hint: Extra image attachment is decided by runtime probability, not by LLM action selection.
imageSelector.autonomousRandomEnabled: Enable random autonomous image after reply
imageSelector.randomProbabilityHint: For example 0.5 means an image is attached in roughly half of eligible replies.

presence.title: Presence Simulation
presence.hint: Open/close chat presence hooks. Use this if you want bridge-level presence behavior to feel more alive.

tests.runImageSend: Test: send image to private target
tests.hint: These are runtime checks: one reads a public source and sends private reflection, another generates and sends a test image to the private target.

notifications.oneChannelRequired: At least one communication channel must stay enabled

feature.wipText: This module is temporarily disabled until backend and logic are ready.
